verdicts.reason: The plan robustly addresses all identified correctness risks, including degenerate data ranges, negative values, data truncation, and invalid input handling, with explicit warnings and fallback mechanisms.
The plan explicitly addresses XSS by escaping all user-provided text before SVG insertion and parsing values safely, adhering to existing architectural security constraints.
When an unknown chart type is specified (e.g., `chart foo`), the system silently falls back to a bar chart, which can confuse the user who expects a different type or explicit feedback.
verdicts.required_fix: Display a small, visible warning message below the chart (similar to the truncation or skipped lines warnings) when an unknown chart type is encountered, informing the user of the fallback to 'bar'.
verdicts.evidence: Subtask 8, point 9: "Unknown chart type — `chart foo` with no recognized type (bar/line/pie) falls back to `bar` with a console warning; no crash"

verdicts.reason: The plan includes comprehensive documentation in DECK_GUIDE.md, explicit in-app help via a <details> block, clear examples, and visible, actionable error/warning messages, ensuring high discoverability for both human users and AI agents.
This feature addresses a clear and recurring need for users creating data-driven presentations, integrating charts directly into the markdown workflow with theme consistency.
verdicts.evidence: The ability to define simple charts inline, with automatic theme-matching, significantly reduces friction compared to generating charts externally and embedding images, making markdown-deck a more complete and useful tool for presentations.

verdicts.reason: The automatic theme integration for charts is a strong signature move, making them feel native to the presentation and differentiating from generic chart tools.
verdicts.evidence: Many online CSV-to-chart tools, Chart.js demos

verdicts.reason: The plan explicitly incorporates the `<details>` pattern for structured input documentation and adheres to the `CONSTRAINTS.md` for CSP, showing proactive application of documented lessons.
verdicts.evidence: Subtask 8, item 12: 'Inline `<details>` quick reference (per LESSONS advisory PLAN-escalation 2026-04-24, satisfies the port-ref `<details>` pattern KEEP rule for tools accepting structured input)...' and Security section: 'CSP unchanged (CONSTRAINTS.md)'

objections.reason: When an unknown chart type is specified (e.g., `chart foo`), the system silently falls back to a bar chart, which can confuse the user who expects a different type or explicit feedback.
